title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Go Client Systems · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http spec-aware cache decision notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Private Shared Cache · changed case · Quiz Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Go vendor-release candidate stores a response with max-age=40, ETag "rev-4", Age:4, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
